description: Telegram AI Digest — fetches messages from Telegram channels via Telethon, summarises them with Claude, and publishes a self-hosted HTML digest page. Source messages are rendered inline via Telegram's official post embeds. The page URL is sent to a private Telegram channel. Entry point: python digest.py. Tests: python -m pytest test_digest.py -v.

testingPolicy.rule: Any story touching fetch_messages, build_html_page, or extract_* functions requires BOTH (a) unit tests green AND (b) python digest.py --dry-run run on this machine with the output HTML opened in a browser. For embed/lazy-load stories the browser check is mandatory: confirm embeds actually render and that they inject only on first expand (not on page load).
testingPolicy.rationale: Unit tests verify code paths but cannot catch layout issues, broken RTL rendering, embeds that fail to load, or lazy-load that fires eagerly — only the rendered browser output can.
testingPolicy.checklist: Before setting passes:true, tick off each acceptance criterion against the rendered HTML file and record confirmation in the story's notes field.

userStories.title: Media-aware fetch + source map
acceptanceCriteria: extract_media_info(message) returns ('video', duration_sec) for video messages, ('photo', None) for photos, ('document', None) for other documents, (None, None) for text-only
extract_external_links(message) returns only non-t.me URLs from message.entities; handles None entities; handles MessageEntityUrl and MessageEntityTextUrl; preserves order of first appearance
fetch_messages returns a tuple (message_strings, source_map) — message_strings for Claude, source_map dict keyed by t.me permalink
Video-only messages are no longer silently dropped — they appear in the Claude input with [VIDEO: M:SS] [סרטון]
Video + caption messages appear with [VIDEO: M:SS] {caption}
Image-only messages appear with [IMAGE] [תמונה]
Image + caption messages appear with [IMAGE] {caption}
Each source_map entry contains: text (str), media_type (str|None), video_duration (int|None), external_links (list[str])
main() accumulates source_map across all channels into a single dict
All tests pass: python -m pytest test_digest.py -v
userStories.notes: Done in PR #7. Duration crash + per-message timestamp follow-up fixed in commit 5e1576f.

userStories.title: HTML digest page + dry-run
acceptanceCriteria: build_html_page(digest, source_map, end_date) is a pure function — no file I/O, no network calls — returning a complete self-contained HTML string
Page is <!DOCTYPE html> with lang='he' dir='rtl' and all CSS inlined (no external dependencies)
Four sections rendered in order: conflict, politics, world, deep — empty sections omitted
Big news items have: h4 heading, meta line (channel + time), AI summary paragraph, קישור למקור link, and a <details>/<summary> source bubble
Minor news items are a <ul> where each <li> is a <details> element — summary is the headline, expanded content is the source bubble
Source bubbles are collapsed by default
Source bubble shows: channel name (bold), timestamp, original message text, 📹 M:SS label for video or 🖼 label for image, and a פתח בטלגרם link to the t.me permalink
External link from source_map entry used as קישור למקור when present; t.me link used as fallback when not
When a digest item has multiple links (merged messages), one bubble is rendered per link
Telegraph code fully removed: publish_to_telegraph, TOKEN_FILE, Telegraph import, telegraph from requirements.txt
--dry-run argparse flag added: generates HTML, saves to HTML_OUTPUT_DIR, logs path, skips send_message
HTML_OUTPUT_DIR and PUBLIC_BASE_URL read from .env (required)
HTML filename pattern: digest-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM.html (local time from end_date)
Public URL returned: PUBLIC_BASE_URL/digest-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM.html
All tests pass: python -m pytest test_digest.py -v
python digest.py --dry-run runs on the production machine and the output HTML is manually inspected in a browser
userStories.notes: Done in PR #7. NOTE: the hand-built source bubbles and קישור למקור link from this story are superseded by US-005/US-006/US-007 (Telegram official embeds).

userStories.title: README: VPS server setup instructions
acceptanceCriteria: TELEGRAPH_TOKEN removed from all .env examples in README
HTML_OUTPUT_DIR and PUBLIC_BASE_URL documented in the .env setup section with example values
Web server setup section added covering nginx (static-file location block) and Python http.server as options
--dry-run flag documented under Usage: what it does and when to use it
Docker run example updated to mount HTML_OUTPUT_DIR as a volume
No references to Telegraph, Instant View, or telegraph_token remain in README
Feature list updated to describe HTML page output instead of Telegraph

userStories.title: Filter self-mirror links out of Further Reading
acceptanceCriteria: extract_external_links excludes the channel's mirror permalinks (abualiexpress.<tld>/heb<id>), with or without a trailing slash, and with or without a #comments fragment
Detection is by the host + /heb<id> path pattern, NOT by the #comments substring (matching #comments alone is too fuzzy — a real external article could carry that anchor)
Genuine external links (non-t.me, non-mirror, e.g. go.amitsegal.co.il/<code>) are still returned
t.me links still excluded; None/empty entities handled; first-appearance order and dedup preserved
New unit tests cover: mirror permalink excluded, mirror+#comments excluded, genuine external kept, t.me excluded
All tests pass: python -m pytest test_digest.py -v
userStories.notes: Reference: CONTEXT.md (Further Reading Link, Comment Link), ADR-0002.

userStories.title: Replace source bubbles with lazy-loaded Telegram embeds
acceptanceCriteria: Each source renders as an official Telegram embed carrying data-telegram-post='<channel>/<id>' derived from the t.me link
Embeds are NOT loaded on page render; a small inline JS lazy-loader injects telegram-widget.js?23 into a <details> on its first open
The קישור למקור link is removed from big-news items
Hand-built bubble markup and CSS removed (.source-bubble, .bubble-*, פתח בטלגרם, 📹/🖼 labels)
Applies to both big_news and minor_news across conflict/politics/world/deep
build_html_page stays a pure function returning an HTML string (lazy-load JS inlined in the page)
Tests updated: assert embed attributes + lazy-load hook present; bubble-internal assertions removed
All tests pass AND python digest.py --dry-run run on this machine with the HTML inspected in a browser (embeds render, lazy-load works)
userStories.notes: Keep one button PER source in this slice; unifying into a single thread is US-006. Reference: ADR-0002, CONTEXT.md (Source Bubble).

userStories.title: Unify per-source buttons into one source-thread button
acceptanceCriteria: A story merging N messages shows exactly ONE <details> button; expanding reveals all N embeds in chronological order
Lazy-load still applies (all embeds in the thread inject on first open)
Applies to both big_news and minor_news
Tests updated to expect a single button per story
All tests pass AND python digest.py --dry-run inspected in a browser
Implemented as a single isolated commit (independently revertible)
userStories.notes: EXPERIMENTAL — maintainer is unsure a unified thread reads better than per-source buttons. Must be cleanly revertible as its own commit.

userStories.title: Add Further Reading link to big-news items
acceptanceCriteria: A big-news item whose messages contain a genuine external link shows a 'להמשך קריאה ←' link (first qualifying link)
A big-news item with no external link (or only mirror/comment links) shows no further-reading link
Minor-news items never show a further-reading link
The further-reading link is visually separate from the source-thread button
Tests cover present and absent cases
All tests pass AND python digest.py --dry-run inspected in a browser
userStories.notes: Reference: CONTEXT.md (Further Reading Link). Depends on US-004's filtering producing clean external_links.
